description: Change a person, thing, or practice in response to altered conditions while retaining enough of its identity or purpose to continue. Adaptation may be chosen or imposed, helpful or harmful, and successful or unsuccessful; the verb settles none of those questions.
articulatory_notes: Begin with unvoiced 'h' at the glottis and raise the tongue into high-front 'i'. Set the tongue tip against the backs of the upper teeth for voiced dental 'n' and release the nasal air into stressed rounded middle-height back 'o'. Move the tip to the alveolar ridge for 'l' and finish with rounded high-back 'u'.
sound_symbolism: After the light opening, the word changes contact twice while the voice continues through nasal and lateral sounds. That controlled reshaping is a useful phonesthetic companion to adjustment.
usage_notes: The verb may be intransitive or take the thing being adapted as its object. Accessibility-module 'hinawu' (accommodate) specifically answers a stated access requirement. 'molei' (evolve) concerns accumulated development over time, while 'helui' (change) simply reports difference.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Climate adaptation needs a neutral verb. Communities may alter tools, homes, or practices in response to changed conditions, while justice, burden, and actual protection still have to be stated.

semantic_domains.activity: deliberate or responsive adjustment to conditions
semantic_domains.cognition: revision of a method or practice after new information
semantic_domains.nature: organisms or living systems changing in relation to their surroundings
